_note: Near-synonym discrimination. Each item is a frame where only one candidate fits; distractors are the cluster's other members. The 'why' is the boundary the gloss hides.

items.frame: אמרו לו לא לבוא, אז הוא ___ בא.
items.en: They told him not to come, so he came ___.
items.why: דווקא = doing it *because* you were told not to — defiance / contrary to expectation. בעצם and למעשה carry no such spin.

items.en: Wait… ___ you're right. I was wrong.
items.why: בעצם marks a mid-thought self-correction ('on second thought'). דווקא (against expectation) and למעשה (in practice) don't fit a change of mind.

items.frame: הוא מנהל את החברה ___, גם אם לא באופן רשמי.
items.en: He runs the company ___, even if not officially.
items.why: למעשה = 'in practice / de facto'. דווקא and בעצם can't mean 'in actual fact' the way 'de facto' needs here.

items.frame: חשבתי שלא יבוא, אבל הוא ___ הגיע ראשון.
items.en: I thought he wouldn't come, but he ___ arrived first.
items.why: Reversal of expectation — not only came, came first. That surprise-against-prediction is דווקא's core.

items.en: I haven't seen you ___! How are you?
items.why: The collocation מזמן לא = 'haven't … in ages' (up to now). לפני הרבה זמן can't sit in this present-connected negative.
Here מזמן = 'already / long since' marking an elapsed state into the present. לפני הרבה זמן (a finished past point) is ungrammatical with a current state.

items.en: I met her ___, just a week ago.
items.why: לא מזמן = 'recently'. (Note: it's NOT the negation of idiomatic מזמן — it's the *opposite* of 'long ago'.) 'A week ago' rules out the two 'long ago' options.
Standalone מזמן! = 'ages ago!' (often with an implied '…and I've been waiting'). A bare לפני הרבה זמן sounds oddly flat as a reply.

items.en: I didn't come because of the rain.
items.why: בגלל takes a NOUN (הגשם). כי needs a full clause; לכן means 'therefore'.

items.en: I didn't come because it was raining.
items.why: כי introduces a CLAUSE (ירד גשם). You can't say בגלל ירד גשם — בגלל needs a noun.

items.en: It was raining, so I didn't come.
items.why: לכן = 'therefore', marks the RESULT. בגלל/כי point at the cause — wrong direction.

items.en: He's tired because he worked all night.
items.why: A clause follows (עבד כל הלילה) → כי. בגלל would need a noun: בגלל העבודה.
Lowest certainty ('maybe') pairs with 'I'm not sure'. בטח/בוודאי (high certainty) clash with the hedge.

items.frame: האורות כבויים — ___ אין אף אחד בבית.
items.en: The lights are off — ___ no one's home.
items.why: כנראה = an inference from evidence ('apparently/probably'). אולי is a bare guess; בוודאי overclaims given it's just an inference.
בטח as an enthusiastic 'sure! / of course!'. אולי (maybe) contradicts 'gladly'.

items.frame: שתיים ועוד שתיים שווה ארבע — זה ___ נכון.
items.en: Two plus two is four — that's ___ true.
items.why: Certainty about a fact → בוודאי. אולי/כנראה under-commit to something that's simply certain.
ממש intensifies a whole statement/verb ('I really can't…'). מאוד modifies adjectives, not לא מאמין — *מאוד לא מאמין* is off.

items.frame: היה ___ נחמד, אבל לא משהו מיוחד.
items.en: It was ___ nice, but nothing special.
items.why: די = 'fairly / moderately' — matches the dialed-down 'nothing special'. מאוד/ממש (very/really) clash with that.

items.frame: ___ נפלתי, אבל הצלחתי לתפוס את עצמי.
items.why: כמעט = 'almost' — the event didn't actually happen. The others are intensifiers, not 'almost'.
